metadata.description: Literature search for 10 recent (2023-2026) publications from New Media & Society (SAGE) relevant to manuscript on semantic compression of artist stakeholder concerns in AI discourse, topic modeling, AI governance, creative labor, framing theory, epistemic injustice, and algorithmic agenda-setting.

summary: Conducted systematic literature search for 10 recent (2023-2026) New Media & Society publications relevant to manuscript on semantic compression of artist concerns in AI discourse. Executed 30+ web searches across SAGE Journals and Google Scholar. Verified all 10 selected papers via DOI prefix 14614448 (NM&S ISSN). Excluded several initially promising papers that turned out to be in other SAGE journals (Big Data & Society, JMCQ, Public Understanding of Science, Social Science Computer Review, Convergence). Final list spans computational text analysis, AI discourse framing, creative labor, epistemic injustice, algorithmic agenda-setting, and generative AI bias -- all core themes of the manuscript.

decision.description: Compile a list of 10 recent (2023-2026) New Media & Society publications relevant to manuscript on semantic compression of artist concerns in AI discourse. Selection criteria: (1) confirmed NM&S journal via DOI prefix 14614448, (2) thematic relevance to topic modeling, AI discourse, creative labor, framing, epistemic injustice, algorithmic agenda-setting, (3) recency priority 2024-2026.
decision.rationale: User requested literature search for NM&S references for manuscript revision. Papers selected based on thematic alignment and journal verification through DOI prefix and SAGE Journals URLs.

contribution.description: Systematic literature search identifying 10 verified New Media & Society publications (2024-2026) relevant to manuscript on semantic compression of artist concerns in AI discourse. Each entry includes full citation, DOI, summary, and manuscript connection.
